title: Lessons from building Claude Code: How we use skills
preview: Skills have become one of the most used extension points in Claude Code. They're flexible, easy to make, and easy to distribute.

blocks.english: Skills have become one of the most used extension points in Claude Code. They're flexible, easy to make, and easy to distribute.
blocks.chinese: Skills 已成为 Claude Code 中使用最多的扩展点之一。它们灵活、易于创建，也便于分发。

blocks.english: But this flexibility also makes it hard to know what works best. What type of skills are worth making? How do you structure a skill? When do you share them with others?
blocks.chinese: 但这种灵活性也让人难以判断什么最有效。什么类型的 Skills 值得创建？如何构建一个 Skill？何时与他人分享？

blocks.english: We've been using skills in Claude Code extensively at Anthropic with hundreds of them in active use. These are the lessons we've learned about using skills to accelerate our development.
blocks.chinese: 我们在 Anthropic 内部大量使用 Claude Code 的 Skills，目前有数百个处于活跃使用状态。以下是我们关于使用 Skills 加速开发所学到的经验教训。

blocks.english: What are skills?
blocks.chinese: 什么是 Skills？

blocks.english: Skills are folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that agents can discover and use to do things more accurately and efficiently. This blog post assumes familiarity with skills basics; if you're new, start with our Introduction to agent skills course on Skilljar.
blocks.chinese: Skills 是由指令、脚本和资源组成的文件夹，智能体可以发现并使用它们来更准确、更高效地完成任务。本博文假设读者已具备 Skills 基础知识；如果你是新手，请从我们在 Skilljar 上的"智能体 Skills 入门"课程开始。

blocks.english: A common misconception we hear about skills is that they are "just markdown files." They're actually folders that can include scripts, assets, data, etc. that the agent can discover, explore and manipulate.
blocks.chinese: 我们听到的一个常见误解是，Skills"不过是 markdown 文件"。实际上它们是可以包含脚本、资源、数据等的文件夹，智能体可以发现、探索和操作其中的内容。

blocks.english: In Claude Code, skills also have a wide variety of configuration options including registering dynamic hooks.
blocks.chinese: 在 Claude Code 中，Skills 还具有多种配置选项，包括注册动态钩子。

blocks.english: We've found that some of the most effective skills in Claude Code use these configuration options and folder structure effectively.
blocks.chinese: 我们发现，Claude Code 中一些最有效的 Skills 正是充分利用了这些配置选项和文件夹结构。

blocks.english: Types of skills
blocks.chinese: Skills 的类型

blocks.english: After cataloging all of our internal skills at Anthropic, we noticed they cluster into nine categories. The best skills fit cleanly into one; the ones that try to do too much straddle several and confuse the agent. This isn't a definitive list, but it is a useful framework for identifying gaps in your own skills library.
blocks.chinese: 在对 Anthropic 内部所有 Skills 进行分类后，我们发现它们可以归纳为九个类别。最好的 Skills 可以清晰地归入某一类；而试图涵盖太多功能的 Skills 则会横跨多个类别，让智能体感到困惑。这不是一个确定性的列表，但它是一个有用的框架，可以帮助你识别自己 Skills 库中的空白。

blocks.english: The Claude Code team categorized our internal skills and found that they could be bucketed into nine distinct categories.
blocks.chinese: Claude Code 团队将我们的内部 Skills 进行了分类，发现它们可以归纳为九个不同的类别。

blocks.english: 1. Library and API reference
blocks.chinese: 1. 库和 API 参考

blocks.english: These are skills that explain how to correctly use a library, CLI, or SDKs. They could be both for internal libraries or common libraries that Claude Code sometimes struggles to handle. These skills often included a folder of reference code snippets and a list of gotchas for Claude to avoid when writing a script.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 解释如何正确使用某个库、CLI 或 SDK。它们可以针对内部库，也可以针对 Claude Code 有时难以处理的常见库。这些 Skills 通常包含一个参考代码片段文件夹和一份 Claude 在编写脚本时需要避免的陷阱列表。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `billing-lib` — your internal billing library: edge cases, footguns, etc.
blocks.chinese: - `billing-lib` — 你的内部计费库：边界情况、容易踩坑的地方等。

blocks.english: - `internal-platform-cli` — every subcommand of your internal CLI wrapper with examples on when to use them.
blocks.chinese: - `internal-platform-cli` — 你的内部 CLI 封装的每个子命令，附带使用场景示例。

blocks.english: - `sandbox-proxy` — configuring your org's egress gateway for dev work: which hosts are reachable, how to debug "connection refused" errors, how to add an allowlist entry.
blocks.chinese: - `sandbox-proxy` — 配置组织的出站网关用于开发工作：哪些主机可达、如何调试"连接被拒绝"错误、如何添加白名单条目。

blocks.english: 2. Product verification
blocks.chinese: 2. 产品验证

blocks.english: These are skills that describe how to test or verify that your code is working. They are often paired with playwright, tmux, or other external tools for verification.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 描述如何测试或验证代码是否正常工作。它们通常与 Playwright、tmux 或其他外部验证工具配合使用。

blocks.english: Verification skills have had the most measurable impact on Claude's output quality internally. It can be worth having an engineer spend a week just making your verification skills excellent.
blocks.chinese: 验证类 Skills 对 Claude 的输出质量产生了内部最具可衡量性的影响。值得让一名工程师花一周时间专门优化你的验证 Skills。

blocks.english: Consider techniques like having Claude record a video of its output so you can see exactly what it tested, or enforcing programmatic assertions on state at each step. These are often done by including a variety of scripts in the skill.
blocks.chinese: 可以考虑一些技巧，比如让 Claude 录制其输出的视频，这样你就能确切看到它测试了什么，或者在每个步骤中对状态进行编程断言。这些通常通过在 Skill 中包含各种脚本来实现。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `signup-flow-driver` — runs through signup → email verify → onboarding in a headless browser, with hooks for asserting state at each step.
blocks.chinese: - `signup-flow-driver` — 在无头浏览器中运行注册 → 邮箱验证 → 引导流程，每个步骤都有状态断言钩子。

blocks.english: - `checkout-verifier` — drives the checkout UI with Stripe test cards, verifies the invoice actually lands in the right state.
blocks.chinese: - `checkout-verifier` — 使用 Stripe 测试卡驱动结账 UI，验证发票确实到达正确的状态。

blocks.english: - `tmux-cli-driver` — for interactive CLI testing where the thing you're verifying needs a TTY.
blocks.chinese: - `tmux-cli-driver` — 用于需要 TTY 的交互式 CLI 测试验证。

blocks.english: 3. Data fetching and analysis
blocks.chinese: 3. 数据获取与分析

blocks.english: These are skills that connect to your data and monitoring stacks. These skills might include libraries to fetch your data with credentials, specific dashboard ids, etc., as well as instructions on common workflows or ways to get data.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 连接到你的数据和监控栈。它们可能包含用于带凭据获取数据的库、特定仪表板 ID 等，以及常见工作流或数据获取方式的说明。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `funnel-query` — "which events do I join to see signup → activation → paid" plus the table that actually has the canonical user_id.
blocks.chinese: - `funnel-query` — "我需要关联哪些事件来查看注册 → 激活 → 付费"以及实际包含规范 user_id 的表。

blocks.english: - `cohort-compare` — compare two cohorts' retention or conversion, flag statistically significant deltas, link to the segment definitions.
blocks.chinese: - `cohort-compare` — 比较两个队列的留存或转化率，标记统计显著差异，链接到分群定义。

blocks.english: - `grafana` — datasource UIDs, cluster names, problem → dashboard lookup table.
blocks.chinese: - `grafana` — 数据源 UID、集群名称、问题 → 仪表板查找表。

blocks.english: - `datadog` — field reference (@request_id vs trace_id), service list, metric prefix conventions.
blocks.chinese: - `datadog` — 字段参考（@request_id vs trace_id）、服务列表、指标前缀约定。

blocks.english: 4. Business process and team automation
blocks.chinese: 4. 业务流程与团队自动化

blocks.english: These are skills that automate repetitive workflows into one command. These skills are usually fairly simple instructions but might have more complicated dependencies on other skills or MCPs. For these skills, saving previous results in log files can help the model stay consistent and reflect on previous executions of the workflow.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 将重复性工作流自动化为一个命令。这些 Skills 通常是比较简单的指令，但可能对其他 Skills 或 MCP 有更复杂的依赖。对于这些 Skills，将先前的结果保存在日志文件中可以帮助模型保持一致性并回顾工作流的先前执行情况。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `standup-post` — aggregates your ticket tracker, GitHub activity, and prior Slack → formatted standup, delta-only.
blocks.chinese: - `standup-post` — 聚合你的工单追踪器、GitHub 活动和之前的 Slack → 格式化的站会报告，仅显示变化部分。

blocks.english: - `create-<ticket-system>-ticket` — enforces schema (valid enum values, required fields) plus post-creation workflow (ping reviewer, link in Slack).
blocks.chinese: - `create-<ticket-system>-ticket` — 强制执行模式（有效枚举值、必填字段）以及创建后的工作流（通知审核者、在 Slack 中链接）。

blocks.english: - `weekly-recap` — merged PRs + closed tickets + deploys → formatted recap post.
blocks.chinese: - `weekly-recap` — 合并的 PR + 关闭的工单 + 部署 → 格式化的周报。

blocks.english: 5. Code scaffolding and templates
blocks.chinese: 5. 代码脚手架与模板

blocks.english: These are skills that generate framework boilerplates for a specific function in a codebase. You might combine these skills with scripts that can be composed. They are especially useful when your scaffolding has natural language requirements that can't be purely covered by code.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 为代码库中的特定功能生成框架样板代码。你可以将这些 Skills 与可组合的脚本结合使用。当你的脚手架具有无法纯粹用代码覆盖的自然语言需求时，它们特别有用。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `new-<framework>-workflow` — scaffolds a new service/workflow/handler with your annotations.
blocks.chinese: - `new-<framework>-workflow` — 使用你的注解搭建新的服务/工作流/处理器。

blocks.english: - `new-migration` — your migration file template plus common gotchas.
blocks.chinese: - `new-migration` — 你的迁移文件模板加上常见陷阱。

blocks.english: - `create-app` — new internal app with your auth, logging, and deploy config pre-wired.
blocks.chinese: - `create-app` — 新的内部应用，预配置了认证、日志和部署配置。

blocks.english: 6. Code quality and review
blocks.chinese: 6. 代码质量与审查

blocks.english: These are skills that enforce code quality inside of your org and help review code. These can include deterministic scripts or tools for maximum robustness. You may want to run these skills automatically as part of hooks or inside of a GitHub Action.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 在组织内强制执行代码质量并帮助审查代码。它们可以包含确定性脚本或工具以实现最大的稳健性。你可能希望将这些 Skills 作为钩子或 GitHub Action 的一部分自动运行。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `adversarial-review` — spawns a fresh-eyes subagent to critique, implements fixes, iterates until findings degrade to nitpicks.
blocks.chinese: - `adversarial-review` — 生成一个全新的子智能体进行批评审查，实施修复，迭代直到发现的问题变成小问题。

blocks.english: - `code-style` — enforces code style, especially styles that Claude does not do well by default.
blocks.chinese: - `code-style` — 强制执行代码风格，特别是 Claude 默认情况下做得不好的风格。

blocks.english: - `testing-practices` — instructions on how to write tests and what to test.
blocks.chinese: - `testing-practices` — 关于如何编写测试以及测试什么的指导。

blocks.english: 7. CI/CD and deployment
blocks.chinese: 7. CI/CD 与部署

blocks.english: These are skills that help you fetch, push, and deploy code inside of your codebase. These skills may reference other skills to collect data.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 帮助你在代码库中获取、推送和部署代码。这些 Skills 可能会引用其他 Skills 来收集数据。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `babysit-pr` — monitors a PR → retries flaky CI → resolves merge conflicts → enables auto-merge.
blocks.chinese: - `babysit-pr` — 监控 PR → 重试不稳定的 CI → 解决合并冲突 → 启用自动合并。

blocks.english: - `deploy-<service>` — build → smoke test → gradual traffic rollout with error-rate comparison → auto-rollback on regression.
blocks.chinese: - `deploy-<service>` — 构建 → 冒烟测试 → 逐步流量切换并比较错误率 → 回归时自动回滚。

blocks.english: - `cherry-pick-prod` — isolated worktree → cherry-pick → conflict resolution → PR with template.
blocks.chinese: - `cherry-pick-prod` — 隔离的工作树 → cherry-pick → 冲突解决 → 基于模板创建 PR。

blocks.english: 8. Runbooks
blocks.chinese: 8. 运维手册

blocks.english: These are skills that take a symptom (such as a Slack thread, alert, or error signature), walk through a multi-tool investigation, and produce a structured report.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 接收一个症状（如 Slack 讨论、告警或错误签名），通过多工具调查流程，生成结构化的报告。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `<service>-debugging` — maps symptoms → tools → query patterns for your highest-traffic services.
blocks.chinese: - `<service>-debugging` — 为你的高流量服务映射症状 → 工具 → 查询模式。

blocks.english: - `oncall-runner` — fetches the alert → checks the usual suspects → formats a finding.
blocks.chinese: - `oncall-runner` — 获取告警 → 检查常见原因 → 格式化调查结果。

blocks.english: - `log-correlator` — given a request ID, pulls matching logs from every system that might have touched it.
blocks.chinese: - `log-correlator` — 给定一个请求 ID，从可能涉及的每个系统中拉取匹配的日志。

blocks.english: 9. Infrastructure operations
blocks.chinese: 9. 基础设施运维

blocks.english: These are skills that perform routine maintenance and operational procedures, some of which involve destructive actions that benefit from guardrails. These make it easier for engineers to follow best practices in critical operations.
blocks.chinese: 这些 Skills 执行例行维护和运维操作，其中一些涉及破坏性操作，需要安全防护。它们使工程师更容易在关键操作中遵循最佳实践。

blocks.english: Examples include:
blocks.chinese: 示例包括：

blocks.english: - `<resource>-orphans` — finds orphaned pods/volumes → posts to Slack → soak period → user confirms → cascading cleanup.
blocks.chinese: - `<resource>-orphans` — 查找孤立的 Pod/卷 → 发布到 Slack → 等待期 → 用户确认 → 级联清理。

blocks.english: - `dependency-management` — your org's dependency approval workflow.
blocks.chinese: - `dependency-management` — 你的组织的依赖审批工作流。

blocks.english: - `cost-investigation` — "why did our storage/egress bill spike" with the specific buckets and query patterns.
blocks.chinese: - `cost-investigation` — "为什么我们的存储/出站流量账单飙升"，附带特定的存储桶和查询模式。

blocks.english: Tips for making skills
blocks.chinese: 创建 Skills 的技巧

blocks.english: Once you've decided on the skill to make, how do you write it? These are some of the Claude Code team's best practices, tips, and tricks for making skills.
blocks.chinese: 一旦你决定了要创建什么 Skill，如何编写它呢？以下是 Claude Code 团队在创建 Skills 方面的一些最佳实践、技巧和窍门。

blocks.english: Don't state the obvious
blocks.chinese: 不要陈述显而易见的事情

blocks.english: Claude already knows how to code and can read your codebase. A skill that restates what Claude would do by default adds context without adding value. If you're publishing a skill that is primarily about knowledge, focus on information that pushes Claude out of its normal way of thinking.
blocks.chinese: Claude 已经知道如何编程，也能阅读你的代码库。一个重复 Claude 默认行为的 Skill 只是增加了上下文而没有增加价值。如果你发布的 Skill 主要涉及知识，请专注于能让 Claude 脱离其正常思维方式的信息。

blocks.english: The frontend design skill is a great example; it was built by an engineer at Anthropic by iterating with customers on improving Claude's design taste, avoiding classic patterns like the Inter font and purple gradients.
blocks.chinese: 前端设计 Skill 就是一个很好的例子；它由 Anthropic 的一名工程师通过与客户反复迭代，改进 Claude 的设计品味，避免了 Inter 字体和紫色渐变等经典模式。

blocks.english: Build a gotchas section
blocks.chinese: 构建陷阱提醒部分

blocks.english: The highest-signal content in any skill is the Gotchas section. These sections should be built up from common failure points that Claude runs into when using your skill. Ideally, you will update your skill over time to capture these gotchas.
blocks.chinese: 任何 Skill 中信息量最高的内容是"陷阱"部分。这些部分应基于 Claude 在使用你的 Skill 时遇到的常见失败点来构建。理想情况下，你会随着时间推移更新 Skill 以记录这些陷阱。

blocks.english: For example:
blocks.chinese: 例如：

blocks.english: - "The subscriptions table is append-only. The row you want is the one with the highest version, not the most recent created_at."
blocks.chinese: - "subscriptions 表是仅追加的。你需要的行是 version 最大的那一行，而不是 created_at 最新的那一行。"

blocks.english: - "This field is called @request_id in the API gateway and trace_id in the billing service. They're the same value."
blocks.chinese: - "这个字段在 API 网关中叫 @request_id，在计费服务中叫 trace_id。它们是同一个值。"

blocks.english: - "Staging returns 200 even when the Stripe webhook didn't actually process. Check payment_events for the real state."
blocks.chinese: - "预发环境即使在 Stripe webhook 没有实际处理时也返回 200。检查 payment_events 获取真实状态。"

blocks.english: Use the file system and progressive disclosure
blocks.chinese: 使用文件系统和渐进式信息披露

blocks.english: The SKILL.md file points to several other files Claude can reference for specific situations. For example, if a job is pending, it should reference stuck-jobs.md.
blocks.chinese: SKILL.md 文件指向 Claude 可以在特定情况下参考的其他文件。例如，如果某个任务处于挂起状态，它应该引用 stuck-jobs.md。

blocks.english: Like we said earlier, a skill is a folder, not just a markdown file. You should think of the entire file system as a form of context engineering and progressive disclosure. Tell Claude what files are in your skill, and it will read them at appropriate times.
blocks.chinese: 正如我们之前所说，一个 Skill 是一个文件夹，而不仅仅是一个 markdown 文件。你应该将整个文件系统视为一种上下文工程和渐进式信息披露的形式。告诉 Claude 你的 Skill 中有哪些文件，它会在适当的时候读取它们。

blocks.english: The simplest form of progressive disclosure is to point to other markdown files for Claude to use. For example, you may split detailed function signatures and usage examples into references/api.md.
blocks.chinese: 最简单的渐进式信息披露形式是指向其他 markdown 文件供 Claude 使用。例如，你可以将详细的函数签名和使用示例拆分到 references/api.md 中。

blocks.english: Another example: if your end output is a markdown file, you might include a template file for it in assets/ to copy and use.
blocks.chinese: 另一个例子：如果你的最终输出是一个 markdown 文件，你可以在 assets/ 中包含一个模板文件供复制和使用。

blocks.english: You can have folders of references, scripts, examples, etc., which help Claude work more effectively.
blocks.chinese: 你可以设置参考、脚本、示例等文件夹，帮助 Claude 更高效地工作。

blocks.english: Avoid railroading Claude
blocks.chinese: 避免过度约束 Claude

blocks.english: Claude will generally try to stick to your instructions, and because skills are so reusable you'll want to be careful of being too specific in your instructions. Give Claude the information it needs, but give it the flexibility to adapt to the situation.
blocks.chinese: Claude 通常会尽量遵循你的指令，但由于 Skills 的高度可重用性，你要注意指令不要过于具体。给 Claude 它需要的信息，但也要给它适应具体情况的灵活性。

blocks.english: Think through the setup
blocks.chinese: 仔细考虑设置流程

blocks.english: Some skills may need to be set up with context from the user. For example, if you are making a skill that posts your standup to Slack, you may want Claude to ask which Slack channel to post it in.
blocks.chinese: 有些 Skills 可能需要用户提供上下文来设置。例如，如果你正在创建一个将站会报告发布到 Slack 的 Skill，你可能希望 Claude 询问发布到哪个 Slack 频道。

blocks.english: A good pattern to do this is to store this setup information in a config.json file in the skill directory like the above example. If the config is not set up, the agent can then ask the user for information.
blocks.chinese: 一个好的做法是将这些设置信息存储在 Skill 目录的 config.json 文件中，如上面的示例。如果配置尚未设置，智能体可以向用户询问信息。

blocks.english: If you want the agent to present structured, multiple choice questions you can instruct Claude to use the AskUserQuestion tool.
blocks.chinese: 如果你想让智能体呈现结构化的多选问题，你可以指示 Claude 使用 AskUserQuestion 工具。

blocks.english: Write descriptions for the model, not for humans
blocks.chinese: 为模型编写描述，而不是为人类

blocks.english: When Claude Code starts a session, it builds a listing of every available skill with its description. This listing is what Claude scans to decide "is there a skill for this request?" Which means the description field is not a summary, it's a description of when to trigger this skill.
blocks.chinese: 当 Claude Code 启动会话时，它会构建每个可用 Skill 及其描述的列表。这个列表就是 Claude 扫描以决定"是否有适用于此请求的 Skill？"的内容。这意味着 description 字段不是摘要，而是关于何时触发此 Skill 的描述。

blocks.english: It's helpful to include triggers for the skill, like "babysit," in its description.
blocks.chinese: 在描述中包含触发词会很有帮助，比如"babysit"。

blocks.english: Help Claude remember
blocks.chinese: 帮助 Claude 记忆

blocks.english: Some skills can include a form of memory by storing data within them. You could store data in anything as simple as an append only text log file or JSON files, or as complicated as a SQLite database.
blocks.chinese: 一些 Skills 可以通过在其中存储数据来包含一种记忆形式。你可以用简单的仅追加文本日志文件或 JSON 文件来存储数据，也可以用复杂的 SQLite 数据库。

blocks.english: For example, a standup-post skill might keep a standups.log with every post it's written, which means the next time you run it, Claude reads its own history and can tell what's changed since yesterday.
blocks.chinese: 例如，一个 standup-post Skill 可能会保留一个 standups.log 记录它写过的每条帖子，这意味着下次运行时，Claude 会读取自己的历史记录，并能判断自昨天以来发生了什么变化。

blocks.english: You can use the env variable ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} to get a stable directory where you can store data, read more persisting data in skills here: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#persistent-data-directory.
blocks.chinese: 你可以使用环境变量 ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} 获取一个稳定的目录来存储数据，在此处了解更多关于在 Skills 中持久化数据的信息：https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#persistent-data-directory。

blocks.english: Store scripts and generate code
blocks.chinese: 存储脚本并生成代码

blocks.english: One of the most powerful tools you can give Claude is code. Giving Claude scripts and libraries lets Claude spend its turns on composition, deciding what to do next rather than reconstructing boilerplate.
blocks.chinese: 你可以给 Claude 的最强大工具之一就是代码。给 Claude 脚本和库，可以让 Claude 把它的回合花在组合上，决定下一步做什么，而不是重建样板代码。

blocks.english: For example, in your data-science skill you might have a library of functions to fetch data from your event source. In order for Claude to do complex analysis, you could give it a set of helper functions like this:
blocks.chinese: 例如，在你的数据科学 Skill 中，你可能有一个从事件源获取数据的函数库。为了让 Claude 进行复杂分析，你可以给它一组这样的辅助函数：

blocks.english: Claude can then generate scripts on the fly to compose this functionality to do more advanced analysis for prompts like "What happened on Tuesday?"
blocks.chinese: Claude 随后可以即时生成脚本来组合这些功能，以回答诸如"周二发生了什么？"这样的提示进行更高级的分析。

blocks.english: Use on-demand hooks
blocks.chinese: 使用按需钩子

blocks.english: Skills can include hooks that are only activated when the skill is called, and that only last for the duration of the session. Use this for more opinionated hooks that you don't want to run all the time, but are extremely useful sometimes.
blocks.chinese: Skills 可以包含仅在 Skill 被调用时激活、且仅在会话期间持续的钩子。对于你不想一直运行、但有时非常有用的更具强制性的钩子，可以使用这种方式。

blocks.english: For example:
blocks.chinese: 例如：

blocks.english: - `/careful` — blocks rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, kubectl delete via PreToolUse matcher on Bash. You only want this when you know you're touching prod — having it always on would drive you insane.
blocks.chinese: - `/careful` — 通过 Bash 上的 PreToolUse 匹配器阻止 rm -rf、DROP TABLE、force-push、kubectl delete。你只在知道自己在操作生产环境时才需要这个——一直开着会让你抓狂。

blocks.english: - `/freeze` — blocks any Edit/Write that's not in a specific directory. Useful during debugging: "I want to add logs but I keep accidentally 'fixing' unrelated code."
blocks.chinese: - `/freeze` — 阻止不在特定目录中的任何 Edit/Write 操作。在调试时很有用："我想添加日志但总是不小心'修复'了不相关的代码。"

blocks.english: Distributing skills
blocks.chinese: 分发 Skills

blocks.english: One of the biggest benefits of skills is that you can share them with the rest of your team.
blocks.chinese: Skills 最大的好处之一就是你可以与团队的其他成员分享。

blocks.english: There are two ways you might want to share skills with others:
blocks.chinese: 有两种方式可以与他人分享 Skills：

blocks.english: - check your skills into your repo (under ./.claude/skills)
blocks.chinese: - 将你的 Skills 提交到仓库中（在 ./.claude/skills 目录下）

blocks.english: - make a plugin and have a Claude Code Plugin marketplace where users can upload and install plugins
blocks.chinese: - 制作一个插件，在 Claude Code 插件市场中让用户可以上传和安装插件

blocks.english: For smaller teams working across relatively few repos, checking your skills into repos works well. But every skill that is checked in also adds a little bit to the context of the model. As you scale, an internal plugin marketplace allows you to distribute skills and let your team decide which ones to install, as well as include a setup flow.
blocks.chinese: 对于在相对较少的仓库中工作的小团队来说，将 Skills 提交到仓库中效果很好。但每个提交的 Skill 也会为模型的上下文增加一点负担。随着规模扩大，内部插件市场可以让你分发 Skills 并让团队决定安装哪些，同时包含设置流程。

blocks.english: Managing a skills marketplace
blocks.chinese: 管理 Skills 市场

blocks.english: How do you decide which skills go in a marketplace? How do people submit them?
blocks.chinese: 你如何决定哪些 Skills 进入市场？人们如何提交它们？

blocks.english: At Anthropic, we don't have a centralized team that decides; instead we try to find the most useful skills organically. If someone has a skill that they want people to try out, they can upload it to a sandbox folder in GitHub and point people to it in Slack or other forums.
blocks.chinese: 在 Anthropic，我们没有一个集中决策的团队；相反，我们试图有机地发现最有用的 Skills。如果有人有一个希望人们试用的 Skill，他们可以将其上传到 GitHub 的沙盒文件夹，并在 Slack 或其他论坛中指向它。

blocks.english: Once a skill has gotten traction (which is up to the skill owner to decide), they can put in a PR to move it into the marketplace.
blocks.chinese: 一旦一个 Skill 获得了关注（这由 Skill 所有者决定），他们可以提交 PR 将其移入市场。

blocks.english: Composing skills
blocks.chinese: 组合 Skills

blocks.english: You may want to have skills that depend on each other. For example, you may have a file upload skill that uploads a file, and a CSV generation skill that makes a CSV and uploads it. This sort of dependency management is not natively built into marketplaces or skills yet, but you can just reference other skills by name, and the model will invoke them if they are installed.
blocks.chinese: 你可能希望让 Skills 之间相互依赖。例如，你可能有一个上传文件的文件上传 Skill 和一个生成 CSV 并上传的 CSV 生成 Skill。这种依赖管理目前还没有内置到市场或 Skills 中，但你可以通过名称引用其他 Skills，如果它们已安装，模型会调用它们。

blocks.english: Measuring skills
blocks.chinese: 衡量 Skills

blocks.english: To understand how a skill is doing, we use a PreToolUse hook that lets us log skill usage within the company (example code here). This means we can find skills that are popular or are undertriggering compared to our expectations.
blocks.chinese: 为了了解一个 Skill 的表现如何，我们使用 PreToolUse 钩子来记录公司内的 Skill 使用情况（示例代码在这里）。这意味着我们可以发现哪些 Skills 很受欢迎，或者哪些 Skills 的触发频率低于预期。

blocks.english: Get started
blocks.chinese: 开始使用

blocks.english: Skills best practices are still evolving. Most of our best skills began as a few lines and a single gotcha, then got better because people kept adding to them as Claude hit new edge cases.
blocks.chinese: Skills 的最佳实践仍在不断演进。我们最好的 Skills 大多始于几行代码和一个陷阱提醒，然后随着人们在 Claude 遇到新的边界情况时不断添加内容而变得更好。

blocks.english: The best way to understand skills is to get started, experiment, and see what works for you.
blocks.chinese: 理解 Skills 的最佳方式就是开始使用、尝试实验，看看什么对你有效。

blocks.english: - Check out our skills documentation
blocks.chinese: - 查看我们的 Skills 文档

blocks.english: - Find example skills to customize
blocks.chinese: - 查找可自定义的示例 Skills

blocks.english: This article was written by Thariq Shihipar, a member of technical staff at Anthropic, working on Claude Code.
blocks.chinese: 本文由 Anthropic 技术团队成员 Thariq Shihipar 撰写，他致力于 Claude Code 的开发工作。
